workflow_name: QBR Transcript -> Health + Whitespace + Renewal Risk
user_message: Just got this from Kevin Lee at Fabrikam Residences -- I need help figuring out escalation triage. Need this before my 2pm call.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Fabrikam Residences
Date: 2024-02-19
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Michelle (Fabrikam Residences)

[00:02:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on escalation triage from our last session.

[00:08:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:10:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:14:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Michelle (Fabrikam Residences): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:24:00] Michelle (Fabrikam Residences): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:28:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:33:00] Michelle (Fabrikam Residences): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Fabrikam Residences: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF45-N trap (Whitespace hypotheses generated per QBR when only some are warranted); produces complete Health delta assessment, whitespace hypotheses, renewal risk with confidence, action distribution wi
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF45-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF45-N failure mode (Whitespace hypotheses generated per QBR when only some are warranted)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
